notes: Gold consumable rules for occupation_profiles table. 832 rows, grain = soc_code. GRW mean target widened to 4.5-6.5 per EDA (actual 5.321, spec target 5.5-6.5 too tight). Market score bucket 10 is structurally absent -- do not require. data_completeness actual value set is {0.75, 1.0} not the full theoretical set. Golden dataset rule DEFERRED pending golden dataset creation by @primary-agent; spec error on golden dataset #3 noted (29-1215 has wage data, use 29-1211 instead). Concept normalization patterns (CONS-COLLISION-RESOLVED, CONS-COVERAGE-FLOOR) do not apply -- single-source spec with no concept normalization.

rules.name: Grain uniqueness: soc_code
rules.sql: SELECT soc_code, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM consumable.occupation_profiles GROUP BY soc_code HAVING cnt > 1
rules.description: The declared grain is one row per soc_code. Any duplicate indicates a promote or transformer failure. Maps to patterns CONS-GRAIN-UNIQUE and ADV-GRAIN-UNIQUE.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 832 distinct soc_code values across 832 rows (100% unique). Silver DQ rule SLV-OOH-001 confirms 0 duplicates. Logical model: 'Zero duplicates on soc_code. Enforced at promote time.' Threshold: 100% unique.

rules.name: Record ID uniqueness and not null
rules.sql: SELECT record_id, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM consumable.occupation_profiles GROUP BY record_id HAVING cnt > 1 UNION ALL SELECT 'NULL_CHECK' AS record_id, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM consumable.occupation_profiles WHERE record_id IS NULL HAVING cnt > 0
rules.description: Surrogate key record_id (computed via compute_grain_id with prefix 'op') must be non-null and unique. Duplicate or null record_id indicates a grain hash collision or derivation bug.
rules.rationale: Logical model: 'record_id is PK, NOT NULL.' Deterministic SHA-256 hash of natural key (soc_code) should produce 0 collisions for 832 rows. Physical model: 'PRIMARY KEY' constraint on record_id.

rules.name: Row count exactly 832
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN COUNT(*) = 832 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM consumable.occupation_profiles
rules.description: Gold table must contain exactly 832 rows -- one per occupation from Silver base.bls_ooh. No rows added or dropped during Gold transformation.
rules.rationale: EDA report: '832 rows in Silver source. All rows carried forward, no filtering.' Spec: 'Grain: soc_code (one row per occupation, 832 rows).' Silver DQ rule SLV-OOH-003 enforces exact 832 count. This is a 1:1 carry-forward.

rules.name: SOC code format: XX-XXXX
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.occupation_profiles WHERE soc_code IS NULL OR soc_code !~ '^\d{2}-\d{4}$'
rules.description: Every soc_code must match the SOC standard format XX-XXXX. This is the primary join key for O*NET and the CIP-SOC crosswalk.
rules.rationale: Silver DQ rule SLV-OOH-002 validates this format on all 832 rows with 0 violations. Physical model CHECK constraint: soc_code ~ '^\d{2}-\d{4}$'. Carried from Silver verbatim.

rules.name: GRW score range: 1.0-10.0 for all non-null
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.occupation_profiles WHERE grw_score IS NOT NULL AND (grw_score < 1.0 OR grw_score > 10.0)
rules.description: grw_score must be between 1.0 and 10.0 inclusive for all non-null rows. Values outside this range indicate a piecewise linear function error. Maps to CONS-IMPOSSIBLE-VALUE pattern.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'grw_score range: 1.000 - 9.997. All 832 non-null values within [1.0, 10.0].' Physical model CHECK: grw_score >= 1.0 AND grw_score <= 10.0. The piecewise function is designed to clamp at 1.0 floor and 10.0 ceiling.

rules.name: GRW score null count: 0
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.occupation_profiles WHERE grw_score IS NULL
rules.description: grw_score should be non-null for all 832 rows because employment_change_pct has 0 nulls in the source data.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'N = 832 (0 null -- employment_change_pct has no nulls).' grw_score is null only if employment_change_pct is null, which is 0 rows currently. All 832 rows should have a computed grw_score.

rules.name: GRW score rounded range: 1-10 integer
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.occupation_profiles WHERE grw_score_rounded IS NOT NULL AND (grw_score_rounded < 1 OR grw_score_rounded > 10)
rules.description: grw_score_rounded must be an integer between 1 and 10 inclusive. Maps to CONS-IMPOSSIBLE-VALUE pattern.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'All 10 buckets (1-10) are populated.' Physical model CHECK: grw_score_rounded >= 1 AND grw_score_rounded <= 10. ROUND of a value in [1.0, 10.0] always produces a value in [1, 10].

rules.name: GRW score rounded = ROUND(grw_score) exact match
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.occupation_profiles WHERE grw_score IS NOT NULL AND grw_score_rounded != ROUND(grw_score)::INTEGER
rules.description: grw_score_rounded must exactly equal ROUND(grw_score) for all rows. Any mismatch indicates a derivation bug or inconsistent rounding mode. Maps to ADV-CROSS-COLUMN pattern.
rules.rationale: Spec derivation: 'grw_score_rounded = ROUND(grw_score).' EDA: 'DuckDB ROUND is standard (round half up). This is deterministic across all integer boundaries.' 15 boundary occupations at exact .5 breakpoints confirmed consistent.

rules.name: Market score range: 1.0-10.0 for all non-null
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.occupation_profiles WHERE market_score IS NOT NULL AND (market_score < 1.0 OR market_score > 10.0)
rules.description: market_score must be between 1.0 and 10.0 inclusive for all non-null rows. Maps to CONS-IMPOSSIBLE-VALUE pattern.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'market_score range: 1.017 - 9.239. All 832 non-null values within [1.0, 10.0].' Physical model CHECK: market_score >= 1.0 AND market_score <= 10.0. Formula: 0.6*grw_score + 0.4*openings_score, both on 1-10 scale.

rules.name: Market score null count: 0
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.occupation_profiles WHERE market_score IS NULL
rules.description: market_score should be non-null for all 832 rows because both input components (grw_score and openings_annual_avg) have 0 nulls.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'N = 832 (0 null -- both components are fully populated).' market_score is null only if grw_score or openings_annual_avg is null, which is 0 rows currently.

rules.name: Market score rounded range: 1-10 integer
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.occupation_profiles WHERE market_score_rounded IS NOT NULL AND (market_score_rounded < 1 OR market_score_rounded > 10)
rules.description: market_score_rounded must be an integer between 1 and 10 inclusive. Maps to CONS-IMPOSSIBLE-VALUE pattern.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'market_score_rounded actual: 1-9 (bucket 10 absent).' Physical model CHECK: market_score_rounded >= 1 AND market_score_rounded <= 10. Values 1-9 observed; 10 is structurally absent but still valid if it occurred.

rules.name: Market score rounded = ROUND(market_score) exact match
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.occupation_profiles WHERE market_score IS NOT NULL AND market_score_rounded != ROUND(market_score)::INTEGER
rules.description: market_score_rounded must exactly equal ROUND(market_score) for all rows. Any mismatch indicates a derivation bug. Maps to ADV-CROSS-COLUMN pattern.
rules.rationale: Spec derivation: 'market_score_rounded = ROUND(market_score).' Same rounding consistency requirement as grw_score_rounded (GLD-OP-008).

rules.name: Wage percentile overall range: 0.0-1.0 when non-null
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.occupation_profiles WHERE wage_percentile_overall IS NOT NULL AND (wage_percentile_overall < 0.0 OR wage_percentile_overall > 1.0)
rules.description: PERCENT_RANK always produces values in [0.0, 1.0]. Any value outside this range indicates a computation error. Maps to CONS-IMPOSSIBLE-VALUE pattern.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'wage_percentile_overall range: 0.0000 - 1.0000.' Physical model CHECK: wage_percentile_overall >= 0.0 AND wage_percentile_overall <= 1.0. Mathematical range of PERCENT_RANK. Zero violations.

rules.name: Wage percentile overall null count: exactly 23
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN COUNT(*) = 23 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM consumable.occupation_profiles WHERE wage_percentile_overall IS NULL
rules.description: Exactly 23 occupations should have null wage_percentile_overall, corresponding to the 23 null-wage occupations. CRITICAL: nulls must be excluded from PERCENT_RANK, not included as zero.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'Null count = 23 (confirmed exactly 23). Equals null-wage count.' EDA CRITICAL WARNING: if nulls participate in PERCENT_RANK window, DuckDB orders them at ~percentile 0.185, corrupting all positions.

rules.name: Wage percentile education tier range: 0.0-1.0 when non-null
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.occupation_profiles WHERE wage_percentile_education_tier IS NOT NULL AND (wage_percentile_education_tier < 0.0 OR wage_percentile_education_tier > 1.0)
rules.description: PERCENT_RANK within education tier must be in [0.0, 1.0]. Maps to CONS-IMPOSSIBLE-VALUE pattern.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'wage_percentile_education_tier range 0.0-1.0 confirmed across all 8 tiers.' Physical model CHECK constraint confirmed.

rules.name: Wage percentile education tier null count: exactly 23
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN COUNT(*) = 23 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM consumable.occupation_profiles WHERE wage_percentile_education_tier IS NULL
rules.description: Exactly 23 occupations should have null wage_percentile_education_tier, same as wage_percentile_overall.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'wage_percentile_education_tier null count = 23. Equals null-wage count.' Same null population as wage_percentile_overall -- both derived from median_annual_wage.

rules.name: Wage tier valid values
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.occupation_profiles WHERE wage_tier IS NOT NULL AND wage_tier NOT IN ('low', 'below_average', 'above_average', 'high', 'very_high')
rules.description: When non-null, wage_tier must be one of exactly 5 values. Maps to CONS-IMPOSSIBLE-VALUE pattern.
rules.rationale: EDA report: '5 tier values confirmed: low (202), below_average (202), above_average (202), high (122), very_high (81).' Physical model CHECK: wage_tier IN ('low', 'below_average', 'above_average', 'high', 'very_high').

rules.name: Wage tier null count: exactly 23
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN COUNT(*) = 23 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM consumable.occupation_profiles WHERE wage_tier IS NULL
rules.description: Exactly 23 occupations should have null wage_tier, corresponding to the 23 null-wage occupations.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'wage_tier null count = 23. Equals null-wage count.' Wage tier is bucketed from wage_percentile_overall, which is null for the same 23 rows.

rules.name: Wage tier null = wage_available False (exact correspondence)
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.occupation_profiles WHERE (wage_tier IS NULL AND wage_available = True) OR (wage_tier IS NOT NULL AND wage_available = False)
rules.description: wage_tier must be null if and only if wage_available is False. Any mismatch indicates a derivation bug in either wage_tier or wage_available. Maps to ADV-CROSS-COLUMN pattern.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'Wage Tier vs Confidence Tier: The correspondence is exact: wage_tier IS NULL iff wage_available = False.' Physical model: 'Exact correspondence: wage_tier IS NULL iff wage_available = False.' 23 rows both ways, 0 mismatches.

rules.name: Confidence tier not null
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.occupation_profiles WHERE confidence_tier IS NULL
rules.description: Every row must have a confidence_tier assigned. This is a required field with no nulls allowed.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'confidence_tier: every row assigned, 0 nulls.' Logical model: 'confidence_tier IS NOT NULL.' Physical model: NOT NULL constraint. All 832 rows receive a tier classification.

rules.name: Confidence tier valid values
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.occupation_profiles WHERE confidence_tier NOT IN ('high', 'medium', 'low')
rules.description: confidence_tier must be one of exactly 3 values: 'high', 'medium', 'low'. Maps to CONS-IMPOSSIBLE-VALUE pattern.
rules.rationale: EDA report: '3 values only: high (735), medium (74), low (23).' Physical model CHECK: confidence_tier IN ('high', 'medium', 'low'). Logical model defines exactly 3 mutually exclusive tiers.

rules.name: Confidence tier 'low' count: exactly 23
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN COUNT(*) = 23 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM consumable.occupation_profiles WHERE confidence_tier = 'low'
rules.description: Exactly 23 occupations must have confidence_tier = 'low', corresponding to the 23 null-wage occupations. This includes 3 occupations that are also catchall (27-2099, 29-1229, 29-1249) where wage_available=False takes priority.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'low = 23 (2.8%). All null-wage (20 non-catchall + 3 catchall). wage_available = False takes priority per spec tiering logic.' Exact match with null-wage count confirmed.

rules.name: Confidence tier 'high' is majority
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM consumable.occupation_profiles WHERE confidence_tier = 'high') > (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM consumable.occupation_profiles WHERE confidence_tier = 'medium') AND (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM consumable.occupation_profiles WHERE confidence_tier = 'high') > (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM consumable.occupation_profiles WHERE confidence_tier = 'low') THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation
rules.description: The 'high' confidence tier must be the majority -- occupations with wage data that are neither broad nor catchall should dominate.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'high = 735 (88.3%), medium = 74 (8.9%), low = 23 (2.8%).' High tier dominates by a wide margin. Spec: 'high should be the majority.'

rules.name: Confidence tier derivation: low iff wage_available = False
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.occupation_profiles WHERE (confidence_tier = 'low' AND wage_available = True) OR (confidence_tier != 'low' AND wage_available = False)
rules.description: confidence_tier = 'low' must correspond exactly to wage_available = False, regardless of broad/catchall flags. The spec defines wage_available = False as taking priority in the CASE logic. Maps to ADV-CROSS-COLUMN pattern.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'Confidence Tier edge case: 3 occupations (27-2099, 29-1229, 29-1249) are both null-wage AND catchall. Per spec: wage_available = False takes priority. All 3 go to low.' All 23 low-confidence rows are exactly the null-wage rows.

rules.name: backs_stats: 'ERN,GRW' for all rows
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.occupation_profiles WHERE backs_stats IS NULL OR backs_stats != 'ERN,GRW'
rules.description: FutureProof stat mapping field must be exactly 'ERN,GRW' for all 832 rows. Static constant value.
rules.rationale: Spec: 'Always ERN,GRW for this data product.' Physical model CHECK: backs_stats = 'ERN,GRW'. NOT NULL constraint. This is a static field, any deviation is a transformer bug.

rules.name: backs_bosses: 'Market,Ceiling' for all rows
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.occupation_profiles WHERE backs_bosses IS NULL OR backs_bosses != 'Market,Ceiling'
rules.description: FutureProof boss fight mapping field must be exactly 'Market,Ceiling' for all 832 rows. Static constant value.
rules.rationale: Spec: 'Always Market,Ceiling for this data product.' Physical model CHECK: backs_bosses = 'Market,Ceiling'. NOT NULL constraint. This is a static field, any deviation is a transformer bug.

rules.name: Data completeness not null
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.occupation_profiles WHERE data_completeness IS NULL
rules.description: data_completeness must be computed for every row. No nulls allowed.
rules.rationale: Logical model: 'data_completeness IS NOT NULL.' Physical model: NOT NULL constraint. Always computable from the 4 core fields regardless of their null status.

rules.name: Data completeness value set: {0.75, 1.0} only
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.occupation_profiles WHERE ROUND(data_completeness, 2) NOT IN (0.75, 1.0)
rules.description: In current data, data_completeness can only be 0.75 or 1.0 because the only nullable core field is median_annual_wage. The spec lists {0.0, 0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 1.0} as theoretical, but EDA confirms only {0.75, 1.0} appear. Maps to CONS-IMPOSSIBLE-VALUE pattern.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'Only 2 distinct completeness values appear: {0.75, 1.0}. employment_current, employment_change_pct, and openings_annual_avg are all 100% non-null, so the only variation comes from median_annual_wage.' 0.75 count = 23 (null-wage), 1.0 count = 809.

rules.name: Data completeness 0.75 count: exactly 23
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN COUNT(*) = 23 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM consumable.occupation_profiles WHERE ROUND(data_completeness, 2) = 0.75
rules.description: Exactly 23 rows should have data_completeness = 0.75, corresponding to the 23 null-wage occupations. Maps to ADV-CROSS-COLUMN pattern.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'The 0.75 completeness rows exactly equal the 23 null-wage occupations. This is a tautological relationship: the only path to completeness < 1.0 is a null wage.'

rules.name: Null propagation: wage percentiles null iff wage_available = False
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.occupation_profiles WHERE (wage_available = False AND (wage_percentile_overall IS NOT NULL OR wage_percentile_education_tier IS NOT NULL)) OR (wage_available = True AND (wage_percentile_overall IS NULL OR wage_percentile_education_tier IS NULL))
rules.description: wage_percentile_overall and wage_percentile_education_tier must both be null when wage_available = False, and both non-null when wage_available = True. Maps to ADV-CROSS-COLUMN pattern.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'All 23 low-confidence occupations have null wage percentiles. All 809 wage-available occupations have non-null percentiles. The correspondence is exact.' Logical model: 'Null if wage unavailable (23 rows).'

rules.name: NOT NULL fields: identity and classification
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.occupation_profiles WHERE soc_code IS NULL OR occupation_title IS NULL OR soc_major_group IS NULL OR soc_major_group_name IS NULL OR broad_occupation_flag IS NULL OR catchall_flag IS NULL OR growth_category IS NULL OR wage_available IS NULL OR source_load_date IS NULL OR promoted_at IS NULL
rules.description: All NOT NULL fields from the physical model must be populated. This covers identity fields, classification flags, growth_category, wage_available, and metadata timestamps.
rules.rationale: Physical model defines 18 NOT NULL columns. This rule validates the core set that is carried from Silver (all 100% non-null per Silver DQ rules) plus pipeline metadata fields.

rules.name: GRW score mean within 4.5-6.5
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN AVG(grw_score) BETWEEN 4.5 AND 6.5 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM consumable.occupation_profiles WHERE grw_score IS NOT NULL
rules.description: The mean GRW score should fall between 4.5 and 6.5, reflecting the national average growth (~4%) placing at approximately 6.0 on the scale. Range widened from spec's 5.5-6.5 per EDA recommendation.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'grw_score mean = 5.321. The spec target of 5.5-6.5 is too tight; recommend widening to 4.5-6.5 to account for the left-skew from declining occupations pulling the mean below median.' Actual value (5.321) is 0.18 below the spec's low end but within the widened range.

rules.name: GRW score bucket coverage: >= 8 of 10 integer buckets
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN COUNT(DISTINCT grw_score_rounded) >= 8 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM consumable.occupation_profiles WHERE grw_score_rounded IS NOT NULL
rules.description: GRW score should populate at least 8 of 10 integer buckets (1-10), ensuring adequate spread across the scale. ADV-DISTRIBUTION-VARIANCE pattern.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'All 10 buckets (1-10) are populated. Spec requires at least 8.' Current: 10 of 10. Threshold of 8 provides buffer for data source changes.

rules.name: Market score standard deviation > 1.0
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN STDDEV_SAMP(market_score) > 1.0 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM consumable.occupation_profiles WHERE market_score IS NOT NULL
rules.description: Market score distribution must not cluster excessively. Standard deviation above 1.0 ensures meaningful differentiation across the 1-10 scale. ADV-DISTRIBUTION-VARIANCE pattern.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'StdDev = 1.607 (well above the 1.0 minimum). The blended 0.6*grw + 0.4*openings formula produces a more centered distribution than GRW alone.' Spec: 'market_score distribution should not cluster excessively (std dev > 1.0).'

rules.name: Market score bucket coverage: >= 7 of 10 integer buckets
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN COUNT(DISTINCT market_score_rounded) >= 7 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM consumable.occupation_profiles WHERE market_score_rounded IS NOT NULL
rules.description: Market score should populate at least 7 of 10 integer buckets. Bucket 10 is structurally absent (no occupation has both top growth AND top openings simultaneously). ADV-DISTRIBUTION-VARIANCE pattern.
rules.rationale: EDA report: '9 of 10 buckets populated. No occupation reaches bucket 10. This is structurally expected -- achieving market_score = 10.0 requires both top growth AND top openings volume simultaneously, which is a rare combination.' Threshold 7 per EDA recommendation.

rules.name: Confidence tier distribution: exact counts
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM consumable.occupation_profiles WHERE confidence_tier = 'high') = 735 AND (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM consumable.occupation_profiles WHERE confidence_tier = 'medium') = 74 AND (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM consumable.occupation_profiles WHERE confidence_tier = 'low') = 23 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation
rules.description: Confidence tier distribution should match EDA findings: high=735, medium=74, low=23. Medium tier = 7 broad + 67 catchall-with-wage. Low tier = 23 null-wage (including 3 that are also catchall).
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'high = 735 (exact match), medium = 74 (exact match; 7 broad + 67 catchall-with-wage), low = 23 (exact match; equals null-wage count). Total: 832.' These counts are deterministic from the input data.

rules.name: Wage tier distribution: quartile alignment
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM consumable.occupation_profiles WHERE wage_tier = 'low') BETWEEN 180 AND 220 AND (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM consumable.occupation_profiles WHERE wage_tier = 'below_average') BETWEEN 180 AND 220 AND (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM consumable.occupation_profiles WHERE wage_tier = 'above_average') BETWEEN 180 AND 220 AND (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM consumable.occupation_profiles WHERE wage_tier = 'high') BETWEEN 100 AND 140 AND (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM consumable.occupation_profiles WHERE wage_tier = 'very_high') BETWEEN 60 AND 100 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation
rules.description: Wage tier counts should roughly align with percentile-based quartiles. The first 3 tiers (25% each) should each have ~202 occupations. 'high' (75th-90th) ~122 and 'very_high' (90th+) ~81. Ranges provide headroom for data changes.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'Quartile breakpoints are very clean: low/below_average/above_average each have exactly 202 occupations. high = 122, very_high = 81.' Ranges set +/-20 around actuals for robustness.

rules.name: GRW score vs growth_category monotonic alignment
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.occupation_profiles WHERE (growth_category = 'declining_fast' AND grw_score > 2.5) OR (growth_category = 'declining' AND (grw_score < 2.5 OR grw_score > 4.0)) OR (growth_category = 'stable' AND (grw_score < 4.0 OR grw_score > 5.0)) OR (growth_category = 'growing' AND (grw_score < 5.0 OR grw_score > 7.5)) OR (growth_category = 'growing_fast' AND (grw_score < 7.5 OR grw_score > 9.0)) OR (growth_category = 'booming' AND grw_score < 9.0)
rules.description: GRW score must align monotonically with growth_category. The piecewise linear function maps to known ranges per category. Any cross-category inversion indicates a formula error. Maps to ADV-CROSS-COLUMN pattern.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'GRW score monotonically aligns with growth category. No cross-category inversion is possible given the piecewise linear design.' Validated ranges: declining_fast [1.0, 2.350], declining [2.5, 3.833], stable [4.0, 5.0], growing [5.0, 7.5], growing_fast [7.5, 9.0], booming [9.0, 9.997].

rules.name: Market score formula consistency: 0.6*grw + 0.4*openings
rules.sql: WITH openings_ranked AS (SELECT soc_code, 1.0 + 9.0 * PERCENT_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY openings_annual_avg) AS openings_score FROM consumable.occupation_profiles WHERE openings_annual_avg IS NOT NULL) SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.occupation_profiles op LEFT JOIN openings_ranked o ON op.soc_code = o.soc_code WHERE op.market_score IS NOT NULL AND o.openings_score IS NOT NULL AND op.grw_score IS NOT NULL AND ABS(op.market_score - (0.6 * op.grw_score + 0.4 * o.openings_score)) > 0.01
rules.description: Market score should match the documented formula: 0.6 * grw_score + 0.4 * openings_score, where openings_score = 1.0 + 9.0 * PERCENT_RANK(openings_annual_avg). Tolerance of 0.01 for floating point rounding. Maps to ADV-CROSS-COLUMN pattern.
rules.rationale: Spec derivation: 'market_score = 0.6 * grw_score + 0.4 * openings_score.' EDA confirms both components are non-null for all 832 rows. This validates the formula was implemented correctly.

rules.name: SOC major group derived correctly from soc_code
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.occupation_profiles WHERE soc_major_group != SUBSTRING(soc_code, 1, 2)
rules.description: soc_major_group must equal the first 2 characters of soc_code. Validates the carry-forward from Silver. Maps to ADV-CROSS-COLUMN pattern.
rules.rationale: Silver DQ rule SLV-OOH-006 validates this derivation. Carried forward verbatim. Must hold for all 832 rows.

rules.name: SOC major group: valid 22 codes
rules.description: soc_major_group must be one of the 22 valid SOC major group codes. Maps to CONS-IMPOSSIBLE-VALUE pattern.
rules.rationale: Silver DQ rule SLV-OOH-004 validates all 22 groups present with 0 violations. Physical model CHECK constraint specifies exact set.

rules.name: Growth category valid values
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.occupation_profiles WHERE growth_category NOT IN ('declining_fast', 'declining', 'stable', 'growing', 'growing_fast', 'booming')
rules.description: growth_category must be one of 6 valid values. Carried from Silver verbatim. Maps to CONS-IMPOSSIBLE-VALUE pattern.
rules.rationale: Physical model CHECK: growth_category IN ('declining_fast', 'declining', 'stable', 'growing', 'growing_fast', 'booming'). Silver DQ validates this. Carried forward verbatim.

rules.name: Median annual wage range (where non-null)
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.occupation_profiles WHERE median_annual_wage IS NOT NULL AND (median_annual_wage < 25000 OR median_annual_wage > 250000)
rules.description: When non-null, median_annual_wage must be between $25,000 and $250,000. Carried from Silver verbatim. ADV-VALUE-RANGE pattern.
rules.rationale: EDA report (Silver): 'Actual range: $30,160 - $238,380.' Physical model CHECK: median_annual_wage >= 25000 AND median_annual_wage <= 250000. Silver DQ uses same bounds. The $25K lower bound provides headroom below observed minimum.

rules.name: Employment change pct range
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.occupation_profiles WHERE employment_change_pct IS NOT NULL AND (employment_change_pct < -50.0 OR employment_change_pct > 60.0)
rules.description: employment_change_pct must be within [-50.0, 60.0] when non-null. ADV-VALUE-RANGE pattern.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'Extreme values: -36.1% (Word processors) to 49.9% (Wind turbine techs).' Physical model CHECK: employment_change_pct >= -50.0 AND employment_change_pct <= 60.0. Bounds provide headroom beyond observed extremes.

rules.name: Broad occupation flag: exactly 7 True
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN SUM(CASE WHEN broad_occupation_flag THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) = 7 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM consumable.occupation_profiles
rules.description: Exactly 7 SOC codes should be flagged as broad occupations. Carried from Silver verbatim.
rules.rationale: Silver DQ rule SLV-OOH-010: 'All 7 known broad codes confirmed present.' Carried forward verbatim, same count expected.

rules.name: Catchall flag: exactly 70 True
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN SUM(CASE WHEN catchall_flag THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) = 70 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM consumable.occupation_profiles
rules.description: Exactly 70 occupations should have catchall_flag = True. Carried from Silver verbatim.
rules.rationale: Silver DQ rule SLV-OOH-011: 'EDA counted 70 titles containing all other (case-insensitive).' Carried forward verbatim.

rules.name: Education code range: 1-8 when non-null
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.occupation_profiles WHERE education_code IS NOT NULL AND (education_code < 1 OR education_code > 8)
rules.description: BLS education level code must be between 1 and 8 inclusive when non-null. Maps to CONS-IMPOSSIBLE-VALUE pattern.
rules.rationale: Physical model CHECK: education_code >= 1 AND education_code <= 8. EDA (Silver): all 8 education codes present across the 832 occupations.

rules.name: Golden dataset validation
rules.sql: SELECT 'DEFERRED' AS status, 'Golden dataset not yet created at governance/golden-datasets/gold-occupation-profiles-bls-ooh-golden.json' AS reason
rules.description: CONS-GOLDEN-DATASET pattern. At least 3 independently verifiable derivation chains must match pipeline output. DEFERRED until golden dataset is created by @primary-agent. Spec notes: use 29-1211 (Anesthesiologists) for golden dataset #3, NOT 29-1215 (has wage data). Expected golden dataset entries: (1) Software Developers 15-1252: grw_score=8.37, wage_tier=very_high, confidence=high. (2) Registered Nurses 29-1141: grw_score=6.4625, market_score=7.8082, confidence=high. (3) Anesthesiologists 29-1211: wage fields null, confidence=low, grw_score=5.825.
rules.rationale: No golden dataset exists at governance/golden-datasets/gold-occupation-profiles-bls-ooh-golden.json. SPEC ERROR: golden dataset #3 references 29-1215 (Family Medicine Physicians) which has median_annual_wage = $238,380 and confidence_tier = 'high'. EDA recommends 29-1211 (Anesthesiologists) as replacement. BLOCKER for spec completion.

rules.name: Wage available accuracy: equals median_annual_wage IS NOT NULL
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.occupation_profiles WHERE wage_available != (median_annual_wage IS NOT NULL)
rules.description: wage_available must exactly equal (median_annual_wage IS NOT NULL). Any mismatch indicates a derivation or carry-forward bug. Maps to ADV-CROSS-COLUMN pattern.
rules.rationale: Logical model: 'True if median_annual_wage is not null. 809 True, 23 False expected.' This is a derived boolean from the null status of median_annual_wage. Must have 100% accuracy.

rules.name: Employment fields positivity (where non-null)
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.occupation_profiles WHERE (employment_current IS NOT NULL AND employment_current <= 0) OR (employment_projected IS NOT NULL AND employment_projected <= 0)
rules.description: Employment counts must be positive when non-null. Zero or negative employment indicates a data error. ADV-VALUE-RANGE pattern.
rules.rationale: Physical model CHECK: employment_current > 0 and employment_projected > 0 when non-null. Domain logic: BLS reports actual worker counts which must be positive.

rules.name: Openings annual avg non-negative (where non-null)
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.occupation_profiles WHERE openings_annual_avg IS NOT NULL AND openings_annual_avg < 0
rules.description: Annual job openings must be zero or positive. 4 occupations have openings = 0 (BLS rounding from thousands). ADV-VALUE-RANGE pattern.
rules.rationale: Physical model CHECK: openings_annual_avg >= 0. EDA: '4 occupations have openings_annual_avg = 0 (BLS rounding from thousands): 29-1024, 29-1243, 51-2061, 51-7032.' Zero is valid; negative is not.

rules.name: No broad + catchall overlap
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.occupation_profiles WHERE broad_occupation_flag = true AND catchall_flag = true
rules.description: No occupation should be both a broad code and a catchall category. These are independent classifications with no expected overlap.
rules.rationale: Silver DQ rule SLV-OOH-012: 'No overlap between broad occupation codes and catchall categories.' EDA confirms 0 overlap. Carried forward from Silver.

rules.name: All 22 SOC major groups represented
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN COUNT(DISTINCT soc_major_group) = 22 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM consumable.occupation_profiles
rules.description: All 22 SOC major group codes should be represented. ADV-ENTITY-COVERAGE pattern.
rules.rationale: Silver DQ rule SLV-OOH-004: 'All 22 major groups present.' Carried forward. Full coverage across the SOC taxonomy is expected.

rules.name: Data completeness derivation accuracy
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.occupation_profiles WHERE ABS(data_completeness - (CASE WHEN median_annual_wage IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END + CASE WHEN employment_current IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END + CASE WHEN employment_change_pct IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END + CASE WHEN openings_annual_avg IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / 4.0) > 0.01
rules.description: data_completeness must equal the count of non-null core fields (median_annual_wage, employment_current, employment_change_pct, openings_annual_avg) divided by 4. Maps to ADV-CROSS-COLUMN pattern.
rules.rationale: Logical model derivation: 'count of non-null core fields / 4.' EDA: 'Only 2 distinct completeness values appear: {0.75, 1.0}.' This rule validates the derivation formula, not just the value set.
